items.q: Which part of the psyche operates on the reality principle?
items.why: Students confuse ego (reality) with superego (morality). The ego mediates between id's demands and reality's constraints. The superego is the internalised moral conscience.

items.q: What is the capacity of short-term memory according to Miller?
items.why: Students confuse capacity (items) with duration (about 18-30 seconds without rehearsal). Miller's magic number describes how many CHUNKS, not how long.

items.q: What does a correlation of -0.8 indicate?
items.misconception: A weak relationship (because it is negative).
items.why: Students treat the negative sign as 'weak'. The sign shows DIRECTION (as one variable goes up, the other goes down); the absolute value shows STRENGTH. -0.8 is strong.
It's Rapid Eye Movement. “Real Easy Memory” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
Correct answer: 90 minutes. “10 minutes” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: If you sleep 9 hours, how many full ~90-min cycles?
items.why: The real figure is 6. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
